prompt.content: Write a short legal memo in a new document explaining what causation test should be used in Anthony Porter's retaliation claim. Use case law and any pertinent regulations to support your position.

rubrics.name: States that University of Texas Southwestern Med Center v. Nassar held that... (ver_8bc71562049a46f4956a2f0e1fed2741)

messages.content: States that University of Texas Southwestern Med Center v. Nassar held that “but for” causation is used to prove Title VII retaliation claims.

rubrics.name: States that, according to Bostock v. Clayton County, the but-for causation... (ver_1e8675925d454281917b7ac3b681e57a)

messages.content: States that, according to Bostock v. Clayton County, the but-for causation test does not require that the prohibited factor be the sole or primary reason for the adverse action.

rubrics.name: State that 29 CRF 1977.6(b) was updated to reflect the Court's decisions in... (ver_3de57e7b92384968bbe8d6c2334c82b8)

messages.content: State that 29 CRF 1977.6(b) was updated to reflect the Court's decisions in Nassar and Bostock.

rubrics.name: States that Porter must prove “but-for” causation (ver_7834b8f5f656447db9c788510777fcde)

messages.content: States that Porter must prove “but-for” causation.

rubrics.name: States that Porter does not need to prove that the protected factor was the... (ver_612d8fdc67cd421f9ff9b4a2777c3cd6)

messages.content: States that Porter does not need to prove that the protected factor was the primary reason for his being fired to prevail.
